chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Slanderous Accusation

text: 5087 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ أَبِي وَلاَّدٍ اَلْحَنَّاطِ أَنَّهُ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "أُتِيَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ بِرَجُلَيْنِ قَدْ قَذَفَ كُلُّ وَاحِدٍ مِنْهُمَا صَاحِبَهُ فِي بَدَنِهِ فَدَرَأَ عَنْهُمَا اَلْحَدَّ وَ عَزَّرَهُمَا".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5087 - It was narrated from Abu Walad Al-Hannat that he said:
Abu Abdullah (as) said: "Commander of the Faithful (as) was presented with two men, each of whom had accused the other of something concerning his body.
Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) waived the legal punishment for both of them and administered disciplinary punishment (ta'zir) to them."
